summary: Gate resolves a manifest member's qualified kind to its bare key before corpus lookup, and reports an unrecognized kind loudly instead of silently checking zero

per.section: Decision: kind identity carries a provider axis

new.description: A `[[member]]` spelled `kind = "claude-code.rule"` is dispatched to the rule package (checked, not skipped); a `[[member]]` whose kind resolves to no built-in/custom definition yields a loud finding, never a silent `checked 0`.

edit.description: At the manifest_corpus build (~794-814): normalize each pre-extracted member's authored `kind` qualified->bare via the provider resolution (`builtin_kind`/`CustomKind::resolve_bare`, identity `<provider>.<kind>`) before keying, so a `claude-code.rule` member reaches the `rule` slice the dispatch loop reads (`corpus.get(&kind.name)`). AND: a member kind that resolves to no known built-in/custom definition becomes a reported diagnostic — the collaboration-rule failure the fork names (a silent skip reads as done). No SDK change: members.ts keeps stamping the qualified identity; the gate is the side that resolves.

tests.asserts: a qualified `claude-code.rule` manifest member is checked against the rule package (not skipped); an unrecognized manifest kind produces a loud finding rather than a silent zero.

acceptance: A manifest member spelled `claude-code.rule` is dispatched to its bare kind's package; an unrecognized manifest kind is a reported finding, never `checked 0`; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Fork `(gate-kind-spelling-and-unknown-kind)` RESOLVED (John 07-04): gate resolves qualified->bare, unknown kind is loud. Shares src/main.rs with GATE-READS-ASSEMBLY — that entry is blockedBy this one (serialized).

summary: SDK `toManifestMember` sectionizes a module-carried member per heading (converging on the importer) and drops the trailing-newline `line_count` +1

per.section: Decision: three carriages, one feature shape

edit.description: `toManifestMember` today emits ONE whole-body `[[member.section]]` with the H1 line inside the body (`sections: [{ heading: headings[0] ?? name, body }]`, emit.ts:258). Converge on the Rust importer: emit one `[[member.section]]` per heading with the heading line split out of the body, so a re-import round-trips (every consumer carriage-blind). Fix the `line_count` +1: `body.split("\n").length` counts a trailing newline as a phantom line — a 47-line body must count 47, matching the importer.
Update the toManifestMember shape assertions to the per-heading sectionization and the corrected line_count; add a multi-heading module-member case proving importer-parity.

tests.asserts: a multi-heading module-carried member serializes per-heading like the Rust importer, and line_count equals the body's real line count (no trailing-newline +1).

acceptance: A module-carried member's manifest shape matches the Rust importer's per-heading sectionization and line_count; `pnpm --dir sdk test` + cargo fmt/clippy/test all green.
notes: Fork `(module-carriage-manifest-shape)` RESOLVED converge (John 07-04) — three carriages, one feature shape; every consumer carriage-blind. The line_count +1 is the trailing-newline bug fileable within this shape. Shares sdk/src/emit.ts with SDK-PLACEMENT-ROUNDTHROUGH — that entry is blockedBy this one (serialized).

summary: Gate reads the SDK-emitted `roster.toml`/`bindings.toml` temper-owned artifacts as the assembly source, so a members-only `temper.toml` stops reporting spurious dangling requirements

new.description: Parse the two temper-owned assembly-fact artifacts — `roster.toml` (the requirement roster) and `bindings.toml` (kind->package) — mirroring the SDK emitter `sdk/src/assembly_artifacts.ts`. Read-only; the gate consumes these as the assembly source when they sit beside `temper.toml`.

An SDK-emitted members-only `temper.toml` plus `roster.toml`/`bindings.toml` checks green — no `requirement.dangling` — and the declared requirements/bindings gate exactly as a spliced-in temper.toml assembly would.

edit.description: Add `pub mod assembly_artifacts;` (the new reader module's foundation line).

In the gate's roster/graph tier: when the temper-owned artifacts (`roster.toml`/`bindings.toml`) sit beside `temper.toml`, read the requirement roster + kind bindings from them as the assembly source rather than only the `temper.toml` layer, so an SDK-emitted members-only manifest resolves its requirements instead of dangling.

tests.asserts: an SDK members-only `temper.toml` with `roster.toml`/`bindings.toml` beside it checks green (no requirement.dangling); the roster's requirements gate as declared.

acceptance: The gate consumes `roster.toml`/`bindings.toml` as the assembly source; an SDK-emitted members-only manifest no longer reports spurious dangling requirements; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Fork `(gate-reads-assembly-artifacts)` RESOLVED (a) (John 07-04) — the artifacts exist by ruling; the gate is the side not yet reading them. Shares src/main.rs with GATE-KIND-RESOLVE — serialized after it.

summary: SDK emit reads the committed projection and rounds install's placement lines through its whole-file re-emit, mirroring the Rust EMIT-OWNED-PLACEMENTS resolution

edit.description: Add a placement-lines predicate that decides which committed-projection lines are install's own (the managed-by note + schema modeline), mirroring the Rust `install::placement_lines`. `projectMember` reads the committed projection at the member's locus and passes those lines into `projectBytes` — which already accepts a `placements` param (project.ts:106-117) never populated at the emit call site — so the whole-file re-emit preserves them by construction.

Thread the committed-projection base dir through the compile/projection call site (`emit`/`compile`, ~452-465) so `projectMember` can read the committed projection its placements resolve from; the double-emit purity check still holds (reads are of committed bytes, not clocks).
Assert a rule whose committed projection carries the managed-by note + schema modeline re-emits with those two lines preserved — byte-identical to the Rust projector's output.

tests.asserts: a member whose committed projection carries install's placement lines re-emits with them preserved (the SDK and Rust projectors agree byte-for-byte).

acceptance: SDK projection parity with the Rust projector holds including install's placement lines (the two projectors agree by construction); `pnpm --dir sdk test` + cargo gates all green.
notes: Fork `(sdk-placement-round-through)` RESOLVED (a) (John 07-04) — mirror the Rust EMIT-OWNED-PLACEMENTS ruling. `projectBytes` already takes `placements`; it is just never populated at the call site. Shares sdk/src/emit.ts with SDK-CARRIAGE-CONVERGE — serialized after it.

summary: Make `Primitive::Field` honor the nested key-path its doc-comment promises — held deferred until a nested-key kind (a JSON-manifest kind) consumes it

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

gate.reason: no consumer — the Fenced half graduated to FENCED-PRIMITIVE (its genre-fence consumer arrived); the remaining key-path half has none. `Primitive::Field` reads a flat `fields.get(key)`, so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read — but no current kind (skill/rule/memory) declares a nested key-path field. Revive when a kind demands it (a JSON-manifest kind's nested key-path field).

edit.description: Make `Primitive::Field` honor the key-path its own doc-comment promises: the impl does a flat `unit.frontmatter.get(key)` in `apply`, so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read. No new vocabulary member — this fixes the existing `field` primitive.

Key-path traversal for the field extractor: walk nested TOML/JSON tables to the leaf before `json_to_feature`, kind-preserving per the `type` lattice (`specs/architecture/10-contracts.md`). Absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve.

tests.asserts: a key-path `field` reads a nested value and is absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve; it preserves the source scalar kind.

acceptance: A nested-key-path `Primitive::Field` reads `a.b.c` over a `Unit` and is absent when unresolved; a KIND.md declaring it reads the intended feature; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. (Deferred: not built until a kind consumes it.)
notes: Narrowed 07-03: the Fenced half graduated to FENCED-PRIMITIVE (consumer arrived). Only the key-path `field` half remains, still no consumer. `Primitive::Field { key }` still flat-reads on disk (kind.rs:836). Shares kind.rs/extract.rs with AGENT-KIND — serialize the two if both un-defer.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document, read through the generic loader and checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

per.section: Decision: package binding is by artifact kind

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; and post-adapter-wave the agent kind is mostly curated data (a KIND.md + PACKAGE.md, both outside build's fence) with near-zero engine code — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (curated — humans author it; outside build's fence), under its provider beside kinds/claude-code/skill and kinds/claude-code/rule with a `provider = "claude-code"` line: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, the declared `yaml-frontmatter` projection format + unit shape, composed extraction over the `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` fields plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it — no engine extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` (curated product territory, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter + loader, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add `"agent"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list so a `[kind.agent]` binding reads as a built-in layer, not a custom kind.
Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const beside SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE and register its kind->package binding. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently via the generic frontmatter adapter; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Declared-adapter architecture: no src/agent.rs IR — the generic adapter projects it. Curated kinds/claude-code/agent/KIND.md + packages/agent.anthropic/PACKAGE.md sit outside build's fence; engine wiring is near-zero. Stays deferred: wrong direction under requirements/satisfies. Shares src/kind.rs with EXTRACTION-VOCAB-GAPS — serialize the two if both un-defer.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/architecture/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the authoring-face manifest).

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB root `package.json` is the *private* flume manifest (≠ sdk/package.json) — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Only `.github/workflows/temper.yml` (CI) exists — no release.yml yet. Parked until a human sets creds up.
